Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents an uploaded image or document for text extraction and analysis within the DocuExtract app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who uploaded the document, managed by an external authentication system. (Relationship: User 1:N Document)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf').

fileUrl.description: The URL or path to the stored document file (e.g., in cloud storage).

uploadTimestamp.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

processingStatus.description: The current status of the document's processing (e.g., 'UPLOADED', 'PROCESSING_TEXT', 'TEXT_EXTRACTED', 'PROCESSING_ENTITIES', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED').

documentCategory.description: The AI-determined or user-defined category of the document (e.g., 'IDENTITY_PROOF', 'BILL', 'MEDICAL_REPORT', 'GENERAL'). This guides further structured data extraction.

extractedTextId.description: Reference to the ExtractedText entity containing the OCR-derived text. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedText)

identityProofId.description: Optional reference to the IdentityProof entity if the document is categorized as such. (Relationship: Document 1:1 IdentityProof)

billId.description: Optional reference to the Bill entity if the document is categorized as such. (Relationship: Document 1:1 Bill)

medicalReportId.description: Optional reference to the MedicalReport entity if the document is categorized as such. (Relationship: Document 1:1 MedicalReport)

ExtractedText.title: ExtractedText
ExtractedText.description: Stores the raw and user-edited text extracted from a Document by the AI-powered OCR.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExtractedText entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document from which this text was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 ExtractedText)

rawText.description: The initial text extracted from the document using OCR.

editedText.description: The text after users have made corrections or refinements, if any.

extractionTimestamp.description: The date and time when the text was initially extracted.

Person.title: Person
Person.description: Represents a person whose details might be identified and extracted from documents like identity proofs or medical reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Person entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the person.

lastName.description: The last name of the person.

IdentityProof.title: IdentityProof
IdentityProof.description: Stores structured data extracted from identity documents (e.g., passports, driver's licenses).

id.description: Unique identifier for the IdentityProof entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this identity proof was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 IdentityProof)

personId.description: Reference to the Person entity associated with this identity proof. (Relationship: Person 1:N IdentityProof)

documentType.description: The specific type of identity document (e.g., 'PASSPORT', 'DRIVER_LICENSE', 'NATIONAL_ID').

documentNumber.description: The unique identification number of the document.

issueDate.description: The date when the document was issued.

expirationDate.description: The date when the document expires.

issuingAuthority.description: The authority that issued the document.

placeOfIssue.description: The location where the document was issued.

Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Stores structured data extracted from billing documents, including vendor details and total amounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this bill was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 Bill)

vendorName.description: The name of the vendor or service provider on the bill.

billDate.description: The date the bill was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the bill is due.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the bill.

currency.description: The currency of the total amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

LineItem.title: LineItem
LineItem.description: Represents an individual item or service listed on a Bill.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LineItem entity.

billId.description: Reference to the Bill to which this line item belongs. (Relationship: Bill 1:N LineItem)

description.description: A description of the item or service.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

lineTotal.description: The total price for this specific line item (quantity * unit price).

MedicalReport.title: MedicalReport
MedicalReport.description: Stores structured data extracted from hospital or medical reports, including patient and diagnostic details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalReport entity.

documentId.description: Reference to the original Document from which this medical report was extracted. (Relationship: Document 1:1 MedicalReport)

patientId.description: Reference to the Person entity representing the patient associated with this report. (Relationship: Person 1:N MedicalReport)

reportDate.description: The date the medical report was issued.

reportingDoctor.description: The name of the doctor who issued or signed the report.

hospitalName.description: The name of the hospital or clinic where the report was generated.

chiefComplaint.description: The primary reason or symptom that led to the medical consultation or report.

overallSummary.description: A general summary of the medical findings and conclusions.

Diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
Diagnosis.description: Details a specific diagnosis identified within a MedicalReport.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Diagnosis entity.

medicalReportId.description: Reference to the MedicalReport to which this diagnosis belongs. (Relationship: MedicalReport 1:N Diagnosis)

diagnosisCode.description: A standardized code for the diagnosis (e.g., ICD-10 code).

diagnosisDescription.description: A textual description of the diagnosis.

dateOfDiagnosis.description: The date when this specific diagnosis was made.

definition.description: Stores individual documents uploaded by a user for processing. Each document includes the `userId` of its owner for direct authorization. This path supports secure listing of a user's documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns these documents.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific document.

definition.description: Stores the raw and user-edited text extracted from a specific document. Each entry includes the denormalized `userId` (owner) and `documentId` for authorization independence and referential integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this extracted text.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: extractedTextId
params.description: The unique identifier for the extracted text entry.

definition.description: Stores details of a person identified across documents by a specific user. Includes the denormalized `userId` as the owner, allowing a user to manage a repository of persons they've extracted details for. This path supports secure listing of a user's identified persons.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this person record.

params.name: personId
params.description: The unique identifier for the person.

definition.description: Stores structured data extracted from identity documents. Each entry includes the denormalized `userId` (owner of the document and this data) and `documentId`. It also references a `Person` record owned by the same user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this identity proof.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: identityProofId
params.description: The unique identifier for the identity proof.

definition.description: Stores structured data extracted from billing documents. Each entry includes the denormalized `userId` (owner of the document and this data) and `documentId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this bill.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for the bill.

definition.description: Represents an individual item or service listed on a bill. Includes the denormalized `userId` (owner) and `documentId` and `billId` for authorization and referential integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and bill.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent bill.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the line item.

definition.description: Stores structured data extracted from medical reports. Each entry includes the denormalized `userId` (owner of the document and this data) and `documentId`. It also references a `Person` record owned by the same user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and this medical report.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: medicalReportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medical report.

definition.description: Details a specific diagnosis identified within a medical report. Includes the denormalized `userId` (owner) and `documentId` and `medicalReportId` for authorization and referential integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document and medical report.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: medicalReportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent medical report.

params.name: diagnosisId
params.description: The unique identifier for the diagnosis.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for DocuExtract prioritizes user data segregation and authorization independence to ensure robust security and simplify rule management. All user-specific data is strictly nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This adheres to the 'Private Data' and 'Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data' access modeling mandates.

**Authorization Independence:** Every document, regardless of its nesting level (e.g., Document, ExtractedText, Person, Bill, LineItem, MedicalReport, Diagnosis, IdentityProof), will explicitly contain a `userId` field (e.g., `ownerId` or simply `userId`). This `userId` field MUST match the `{userId}` wildcard in its path and `request.auth.uid`. This denormalization eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent ownership, thereby preventing broken atomic operations (transactions/batches) and making rules easier to debug. For example, to verify ownership of a `LineItem`, the rule simply checks `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to fetch the parent `Bill` or `Document`.

**QAPs (Query Advantage Points):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** By placing all user-owned data under `/users/{userId}/`, the security posture for all documents within this hierarchy is homogeneous: they are owned by the user specified in the path. This means a `list` query to `/users/S5efNV5jpTPoxYNv6SnAlv3jNPO2/documents` will automatically be scoped to documents belonging to `S5efNV5jpTPoxYNv6SnAlv3jNPO2`. Security rules will then enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all operations (read, write, list), ensuring that users can only access their own data.
2.  **Explicit Ownership:** Each document's `userId` field, coupled with the path structure, provides clear and predictable authorization context. This enables rules to validate `list` operations efficiently by comparing `request.auth.uid` directly to the `userId` in the document (for `create`/`update` operations) or the path (for all operations, implicitly). There is no need for complex filter logic within rules, fulfilling the 'Rules are not Filters' principle.

**Addressing the Error Context:** The reported error indicates a `list` operation failure on `/pending_verifications`. This collection is not part of the explicit user-facing entities defined for DocuExtract and appears to be a system-level or administrative collection. The current data structure design focuses solely on the user-owned documents, extracted texts, and related entities as described. Any `pending_verifications` collection would need a separate, specific security model (e.g., admin-only access) and should not be directly accessible by general users via a `list` operation, aligning with the structural segregation principle for different security postures.
